calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-09-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF LAURA KIRKHAM's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Laura Kirkham's motion for leave to amend her complaint is denied.

Plaintiff seeks to add a breach of contract cause of action to allege that her former employer Persado breached its employment contract with her when it failed to pay her commission for a sales deal with Gap Inc. that Gap ultimately cancelled before paying Persado anything on the deal. The court denies leave to amend to add this new cause of action for two reasons. First, the motion is brought only two court days before trial. Plaintiff offers no explanation for her delay in taking discovery to learn the facts that gave rise to the proposed amendment. Second, the amendment would be futile. Plaintiff asserted the theory underlying her new breach of contract claim in opposition to Persado's summary adjudication motion as one basis for her claim that Persado breached the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. The court has considered the claim and the evidentiary support for it in that context and has found insufficient evidence as a matter of law to create a triable issue of fact that Persado agreed to waive the 12-month requirement for sales deals in connection with Plaintiff's commission plan. (See Order, April 14, 2025, at pp. 8-9.) It would be futile to permit the amendment. (See Melican v. Regents of University of California (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 168, 176 [late and futile amendment properly denied].) To permit Kirkham to amend to assert a claim based on evidence that was rejected at summary adjudication two days before trial would unfairly deprive Persado of the opportunity to move for summary adjudication on the new claim.

Plaintiff seeks to add a violation of Labor Code 2751 as a predicate to her Unfair Competition Law claim. This provision requires employers to set forth in writing the method by which commissions will be paid to employees. The court denies leave to amend because of Kirkham's delay in seeking to amend until just before trial. In any event, amendment would be futile. As discussed in greater detail in the April 14 order, the Gap deal was not eligible for any commission when Kirkham closed the deal because it had a term of less than 12 months, pursuant to Persado's written commission structure. Persado later created a new written commission structure for deals like the Gap deal. Kirkham was not eligible for commission on the Gap deal under either of written commission methods Persado promulgated.

Finally, Kirkham seeks to add a punitive damages claim. Because the court has granted summary adjudication on her wrongful termination claim, and no other causes of action alleged in the complaint give rise to punitive damages, the court denies leave to add this prayer for relief.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
